body:
AdSome people give up dessert in January. I’m definitely not one of those people🍦 

💌 Comment “YES!” for more dessert recipes with a nutritious twist!

Yes, I’m a nutritionist and yes, I still eat dessert. Ice cream everyday? OH GO ON THEN. Sometimes with a lil fibre boost, sometimes just because it’s plain old dessert!!! My current hyper fixation: The Ninja CREAMi

MY CURRENT FAV RECIPES:

🍏 Apple Pie Ice Cream
250g stewed apple (skin on - see video)
200g greek yoghurt (or plant-based alternative)
50ml milk of choice
1 tbsp honey / agave / syrup
1 tbsp ground oats
1 tsp cinnamon
pinch of salt
Method: Blend everything until smooth, pour into your Ninja Creami tub, freeze for 24 hours, then spin on the ice cream setting

🍌 Banana & Vanilla Froyo
2 large ripe bananas
100g greek yoghurt (or plant-based alternative)
100ml milk of choice (I used vanilla soy)
1 tsp vanilla extract
1 tbsp maple syrup
Pinch of salt
Method: Blend all ingredients until silky, freeze in your Creami tub for 24 hours, then spin on the frozen yoghurt setting

🍫 Chocolate Date Soft Serve
250g greek yoghurt (or plant-based alternative)
50ml milk of choice
2 Medjool dates (pitted)
1.5 tbsp cocoa powder
2 tbsp maple syrup
Pinch of salt
Method: Blend everything until smooth, freeze in the Creami tub for 24 hours, then spin on soft serve setting

OPTIONAL fibre boosts:  chia seeds, oats, flaxseed, coconut flakes, cocoa nibs or berries

SAVE AND ENJOY!

@NinjaKitchenuk #CreamiDiet  #NINJACREAMi #healthyrecipes

body:
ad Not bread… make my potato wrap instead!

The ultimate gf and plant based BLT with @ninjakitchenuk - saving time and effort with their containers that you can prep, cook and store in. Making this potato ‘wrap’ took just 8 minutes with the roast function, but you can pretty much cook anything with their airfry, recrisp and keep warm functions 🧚🏼‍♀️

Makes 1 ‘BLT’
For the bacon
1/2 block smoked tofu
1 tbsp tamari
1 tbsp maple syrup
1/2 tbsp balsamic vinegar
1 tsp smoked paprika
1 tbsp olive oil
Salt and pepper

For the wrap
1 small potato
15g vegan cheddar

To build
Handful shredded lettuce
1 tomato, sliced
1 tbsp vegan mayo

Using a mandolin on the thicker setting, thinly slice ½ block of smoked tofu. Add into the smaller Crispi container along with the remaining ingredients. Add the lid, shake the container to mix and place into the fridge to marinade until ready to cook. Snap on the Crispi lid and air fry for 10–12 minutes, flipping halfway, until crisp and caramelised.

Thinly slice the potato using the thinnest setting on the mandolin. Add a layer of greaseproof into the larger Crispi basket, tucking in the edges so it doesn’t fly away! Arrange the slices in a slightly overlapping square pattern to form a flat wrap shape. Sprinkle over the vegan cheddar, season and drizzle with a little oil. Snap the lid on and roast for 8 minutes or until golden and crisp.

Carefully remove the potato wrap from the Crispi once it has cooled for 2 minutes. Spread with vegan mayo, then layer on the shredded lettuce, tomato slices, and tofu bacon. Fold or roll gently to serve.

And the Ninja CRISPi is only £149 on ninjakitchen.co.uk!

#crispisavestheday

body:
We made a protein packed cereal milk sort serve with our new Ninja Creami Swirl🍦.

Recipe:

Ingredients:

• 400ml of milk
- 70g 1 of frosted flakes
- ⁠1 scoop of vanilla protein powder
- ⁠1/4 teaspoon of Xanthan Gum powder.
- ⁠1/4 teaspoon of vanilla essence
- ⁠pinch of salt

Instructions:

Step 1: Combine milk & cereal together and leave to soak for 3-4 hours.

Step 2: Use a sieve to separate the cornflakes from the milk and add the milk to the Creami tub.

Step 3: Mix in in the remaining ingredients to the tub and freeze for 24hrs.

Step 4: After 24 hours, attach the tub to the Ninja Creami and select “Soft Serve” then select “Lite Ice Cream”.

Step 5: Once completed attach the tub to the soft serve dispenser to create your own soft serve swirl and enjoy!

Bonus tip- Garnish your ice cream with more corn flakes for a little added crunch.

@ninjakitchenuk 

#CREAMidiet

body:
AD | Meal prep that actually works with @ninjakitchenuk

This 1.6kg chicken gave me 4 days of crispy, juicy meals - made in one machine, in a tiny kitchen.

The Ninja Crispi air fried it in about 60 minutes, and now all I do is hit “Recrisp” for 5–10 minutes each night. Dinner’s done. Fresh salad on the side. 10 minutes total.

The trick? A killer marinade and knowing when to cover the top with foil if it starts browning too fast.

Here’s the marinade I used
• ¼ cup avocado oil
• Juice of ½ lemon
• 1 tbsp Dijon mustard
• 1 tbsp each garlic & onion powder
• 1 tbsp smoked paprika
• 3 tbsp Italian seasoning
• 1 tsp each chili powder, allspice & black pepper
• 1 tbsp fresh rosemary + oregano
• 1 tsp salt

Roast to 73°C next to the bone and let it rest before carving.
One cook. Four meals. Would you try this?

#CRISPiSavesTheDay

I think I have a new favourite toy @ninjakitchenuk 🤣 this thing can turn almost anything into ice cream! I fear I may have to take up that challenge… 👀 Lemme know if you have any flavour suggestions 🙊

I made homemade cookies and cream ice cream, that was so easy and tasted incredible!
I also made frozen Greek yoghurt soft serve, that has 39g of protein per pint, which also tasted amazing! 🙌

I’ll always believe you can enjoy ice cream every day, no rules, no guilt. What I love about The Swirl is how it makes staying aligned with your goals so easy and flexible, without taking the joy out of dessert 🤌

Recipes below:

Cookies & cream ice cream:
60g caster sugar
20g cream cheese

165ml double cream
250ml whole milk

Frozen yoghurt soft serve:

3 tbsp honey
2 tsp vanilla
50ml whole milk
Toppings: frozen blueberries, melted in the microwave, granola & honey

Enjoy!

AD | As it’s Burns Night this week, we used the Ninja Creami Swirl to put our own twist on a traditional Cranachan dessert 🏴󠁧󠁢󠁳󠁣󠁴󠁿 . Those doing Dry Jan don’t need to miss out as our recipe is alcohol free!

Recipe:

Ingredients:

- 1 cup raspberries (plus some extra for garnish)
- ⁠200ml milk
- ⁠1 tablespoon of honey
- ⁠1 tablespoon of yoghurt
- ⁠1/2 teaspoon of vanilla extract
- ⁠1/4 teaspoon Xanthan gum powder
- ⁠Pinch of salt
- ⁠1/4 cup of toasted oats

Instructions:

Step 1: Blend the milk and 1 cup of raspberries together until smooth.

Step 2: Add the blended ingredients into the Ninja Creami tub along with the Xanthan gum, honey, salt and vanilla and mix.

Step 3: Freeze the tub for 24hrs.

Step 4: After 24 hours, attach the tub to the Ninja Creami and select “Soft Serve” then select “Lite Ice Cream”.

Step 5: Once completed, attach the tub to the soft serve dispenser to create your own soft serve swirl.

Step 6: Add your toasted oats and some fresh raspberries on top of your swirl and enjoy.

Bonus tip: You can drizzle extra honey on top of your swirl if you like it nice and sweet 🍯.

@ninjauk

#CREAMiDiet

body:
4 lunches for £6 ~ Crispi Beef & Broccoli Noodle Bowls 🥦 AD
 
These high-protein noodle bowls are loaded with flavour and couldn’t be easier, thanks to the Ninja Crispi. From storage, to prep, to cleaning- they’ve thought of everything. I love that the base of the crispi containers are designed for reheating, which is then separated by the crisper plate, where you add all of your ingredients that you want a crispy, caramelised result. I love portioning out the individual containers for my meal prep so that all my lunches are ready for the week and I can just grab them from the fridge and cook for 5 minutes. Plus when I’m done I can just throw it in the dishwasher!
 
Ingredients 
500g beef mince (£3.50)
1 head of broccoli, florets and stalks sliced (£0.47)
200g noodles (£1.45)
Fresh coriander, chopped to serve (£0.52)
 
Cupboard Essentials 
For the noodles: 2 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tbsp rice vinegar, 1 tbsp honey. For the beef, 1 tbsp cornflour, 1 tsp garlic granules, 1 tsp honey, 1 tbsp soy sauce. 
 
Method
1. Add your noodles and broccoli to salted water and cook for 5 minutes. Drain and coat in the ingredients for the noodles. Also combine the cupboard ingredients with the beef until fully incorporated. 
2. Divide the noodles/broccoli into the base of the Ninja crisp containers. Add the crisper plate, then add a layer of seasoned beef mince. Use the lid to seal then place in the fridge when cool enough. 
3. When it’s time to eat, simply remove the lid and place the Ninja crisp onto the container. Airfry for 5-7 minutes, then serve with a sprinkle of coriander and enjoy! 
 
@ninjakitchenuk #mealprep #budgetmeals #ninjacrispi

body:
#AD We’re turning a tipper into a tiny home - Day 7 
Although we have now built the frame to go on top of our tipper, we have some repairs that we need to do to make the tipper legal to drive. But because we are working outside and it’s freezing cold, we’re making some pukka coffees using the Ninja Luxe Café to helps us stay warm! This 3-in-1 coffee machine is so easy to use and gives a range of café-quality drinks whilst we are working! We love this coffee machine so much and love making coffees for the people who are helping us on this project! 
@ninjakitchenuk 
#tinyhome #coffee #vanlife #tinyhomebuild foryou
